subject: Computer Networking
title: Social Networks Emerge
description: A 60-second lesson on Social Networks Emerge.

hook.text: Friendster in 2002, MySpace in 2003, Facebook in 2004 — the web became a place to connect with people.

buildup.text: Social networks needed always-on broadband, photo uploads, and dynamic pages. Earlier internet couldn't support them.

discovery.text: Facebook scaled from a dorm project to a billion users by building one of the world's largest data center networks.

twist.text: Social networks made users the content. The business model was selling attention, not selling products.

climax.text: By 2010, social media was reshaping politics, journalism, and culture. Networking wasn't just technical anymore.

punchline.text: Social networks turned the internet into a mirror of society.

quiz.question: What infrastructure enabled social networks to scale globally?
options: Massive data center networks and broadband adoption
